summary: PR #2248 attempted to decompile three grcastle functions in src/melee/gr/grcastle.c and update the fn_801CFAFC prototype in grcastle.h. The PR body and decomp-dev bot report claimed all three functions reached 100% fuzzy match, but the PR was closed unmerged with an issue comment saying it was consolidated into other PRs. The main unresolved theme was pointer math in grCastle_801CD960 and grCastle_801CF750, which the author said was non-trivial to replace because relevant castle GroundVars fields were not named or structurally represented.

key_files.role: Replaced three undecompiled function markers with C bodies for grCastle_801CD960, grCastle_801CF750, and fn_801CFAFC.
Added item forward include and changed fn_801CFAFC from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to a typed void prototype.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: fn_801CFAFC prototype changed to void fn_801CFAFC(Item_GObj*, Ground*, Vec3*, HSD_GObj*, f32)

what_changed.text: grCastle_801CD960 was implemented as a do-while loop over 12 entries, calling grLib_801C9B6C on addresses computed from Ground plus 0xE0 plus i * 0x14.
grCastle_801CF750 was implemented with a PAD_STACK(16), an idx chosen from arg1 == 4, a CollData x34_flags.b1234 check, Ground pointer shifting by idx * 16, and a float add at offset 0xD0.
fn_801CFAFC was implemented with a casted s16 write to gp->gv.castle2.xC4, then conditionally calls ftLib_80086A4C when ftLib_80086960(gobj) is true.
grcastle.h gained the Item_GObj forward include and a typed fn_801CFAFC declaration.
The PR body claimed ninja built clean and fuzzy_match_percent was 100.0 for all three functions; the decomp-dev bot reported 3 new 100% matches totaling +288 matched bytes.
The PR was closed with merged_at null and an issue comment saying 'Consolidated into other PRs'.
evidence_refs: metadata_json: pr.merged_at null and state CLOSED

smart_moves.text: The grCastle_801CD960 solution used a natural do-while loop for a repeated 12-entry operation instead of copying repeated calls.
fn_801CFAFC's recovered signature was propagated to the owning header rather than leaving UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS in the public declaration.
The author explicitly called out remaining pointer-math debt and why it was not a simple field-access cleanup in the current type layout.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a function accesses a repeated inline structure not represented by current GroundVars fields, a matching PR may expose raw offset math as type debt rather than solve it locally; keep the debt explicit and route structural fixes to the owning types.
Recovered function bodies that prove parameter types should update the header prototype and required forward includes in the same review slice.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: grcastle.h Item_GObj include and prototype update

naming_conventions.text: The PR kept address-style function names grCastle_801CD960, grCastle_801CF750, and fn_801CFAFC rather than inventing semantic names.
Local variables used short role-style names such as gp, gobj, cd, idx, and pos where their types/roles were evident.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used PAD_STACK(16) in grCastle_801CF750 to satisfy stack/codegen shape.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: The PR evidence contains a 100% match claim and bot report, but no narrow objdiff excerpt explaining why PAD_STACK was necessary.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used casted pointer arithmetic for Ground offsets 0xE0, 0xD0, i * 0x14, and idx * 16 where the current types allegedly did not model the accessed layout.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: This is a bridge tactic and possible type debt under typed-fields-over-pointer-math, not a general style recommendation.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used a casted s16 store to gp->gv.castle2.xC4 in fn_801CFAFC.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: The evidence does not explain whether xC4 is correctly typed or should be refined.

review_feedback.text: No formal GitHub review comments were present in the slice.
Author comment acknowledged pointer math would be fixed soon.

Author later explained pointer math in grCastle_801CD960 and grCastle_801CF750 was not easy to replace with struct field access because castle GroundVars lacked named fields at the accessed offsets and a proper fix would require shared Ground/GroundVars structural changes.
Final issue comment stated the work was consolidated into other PRs.

follow_up_queries: PR 2248 consolidated into other PRs grcastle
GroundVars castle2 xC4 xD0 xE0 struct fields

notes: Changed files, diff content, PR state, and comments are directly available.
The PR body and decomp-dev bot report support the 100% match claim for three functions, but no objdiff or build log details are included beyond the summarized report.
There were no review comments in the slice, so pointer-math concerns come from author issue comments rather than reviewer feedback.
The PR was closed with merged_at null and a comment saying it was consolidated into other PRs; this intake does not infer the outcome of those other PRs.
The pointer-math structural explanation is author-provided PR evidence; it should be treated as a candidate investigation lead, not an accepted current path fact.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Header prototypes should be updated when a recovered body proves the signature
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2248, recovering fn_801CFAFC included changing grcastle.h from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to void fn_801CFAFC(Item_GObj*, Ground*, Vec3*, HSD_GObj*, f32) and adding the needed Item_GObj forward include.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Keep pointer-math type debt explicit when current structs cannot express a matched access
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2248 matched grCastle_801CD960 and grCastle_801CF750 with raw Ground pointer arithmetic, and the author documented that replacing it would require non-trivial Ground/GroundVars structural changes.

source_update_candidates.title: Investigate castle GroundVars layout around offsets 0xC4, 0xD0, and 0xE0
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2248 suggests castle GroundVars did not have named fields for accesses used by grCastle_801CD960 and grCastle_801CF750: offset 0xE0 behaved like an inline array of 0x14-byte elements, grCastle_801CF750 shifted the Ground pointer by idx * 16 and modified offset 0xD0, and fn_801CFAFC wrote a s16 to gv.castle2.xC4. This should be verified against current source and later consolidated PRs before becoming a path fact.
source_update_candidates.reason: The PR exposes likely struct/type debt, but the slice does not prove the final current layout or accepted field names.

source_update_candidates.title: PAD_STACK uses in matched functions need explicit verifier notes
source_update_candidates.text: grCastle_801CF750 used PAD_STACK(16) while the PR only supplied a general 100% match report. This aligns with the existing matching-tactics-need-evidence standard; curator may consider adding this PR as an example of evidence that is useful but not fully explanatory.
source_update_candidates.reason: This is a standards example candidate only; the current standards already cover the principle.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the PR's pointer-math shapes as preferred style; they are explicitly tied to missing struct fields and non-trivial type work.
Do not claim the PR was merged or accepted; metadata shows merged_at null and the final comment says it was consolidated into other PRs.
Do not infer reviewer intent because there were no review comments in the slice.
Do not treat the Claude-generated note as independent validation of type layout without current-source or later-PR corroboration.
